segments.title: Returning to Mikasa during restoration work
segments.summary: Dr. Clarke travels from Tokyo to revisit Mikasa, the Japanese warship he describes as the highlight of his trip, while repair work is under way.
segments.body: This brief travel update culminates in a return visit to Mikasa. Dr. Clarke frames the ship as an exceptional and deeply personal highlight of the Japan trip, emphasizing the appeal of encountering an individual historic vessel in person. He reaches the site from Shinagawa by rail and notes that the earlier trip videos provide context for the location. The ship is partly obscured by scaffolding and repair activity, which he treats as necessary care rather than a reason to postpone the visit. The moment offers a compact pointer for viewers interested in preserved Japanese naval heritage and the continuing maintenance required to keep a historic warship accessible.

evidence.note: Dr. Clarke travels from Shinagawa, identifies Mikasa at 1:38, and describes ongoing repair work and his return visit.
